description: A changelog entry that records an implementation detail but not the user-visible behavior or migration step, leaving upgraders unprepared.
body: Failure mode: the changelog says what was changed internally (refactor, rename, dependency bump) but not how it affects callers, so users miss a behavior change or required migration. Detection signals: breaking changes discovered only after upgrade; entries phrased purely as internal work; no migration note for a changed default or removed API. Do not log changes only in implementation terms. Safer fallback: write each entry in terms of user-visible impact and required action, call out breaking changes explicitly, and link the migration path.
